description:
You are an integration-developer preparing a lightweight local test-bed for calling
    REST APIs.  
    Your goal is to create an isolated Python environment, install two specific
    HTTP-client libraries, and generate two text artefacts whose exact contents will be
    verified by an automated test.

    1. Create a new project directory named “api-test” directly under /home/user.  
       Final absolute path: /home/user/api-test

    2. Inside that directory, create a Python virtual environment named “env”.  
       The virtual-environment’s activation script must consequently reside at  
       /home/user/api-test/env/bin/activate

    3. Activate the virtual environment and, **inside it**, install the following
       *exact* versions of the two libraries using pip (user-scope installation is
       sufficient):
          • httpx 0.25.1  
          • requests 2.31.0  

    4. After the installation, produce two files inside /home/user/api-test exactly as
       specified below.

       a) /home/user/api-test/requirements.txt  
          – plain UTF-8 text, exactly two lines, each of the form
                <package>==<version>  
          – lines must appear in *alphabetical* order by package name.  
          – Required final content (including line-breaks):

                httpx==0.25.1
                requests==2.31.0

       b) /home/user/api-test/install.log  
          – plain UTF-8 text, exactly two lines, each of the form
                <package> <version>  
          – single space between package and version, no extra spaces or
            trailing/leading whitespace.  
          – Lines must also be in alphabetical order by package name.  
          – Required final content (including line-breaks):

                httpx 0.25.1
                requests 2.31.0

    5. The task is considered complete when:  
       • Directory /home/user/api-test exists.  
       • Virtual environment is present at /home/user/api-test/env/.  
       • Both requirements.txt and install.log exist with the exact contents defined
         above.  

    Keep your command sequence concise; 5–10 terminal commands are sufficient.  
    Do not hard-code paths in the files themselves; only the automated checker will
    read them.  
    When you are done, you do *not* need to output anything in the console—just be
    sure the artefacts exist with the correct contents.
truth:
Expected artefacts for verification:

    1. Directory /home/user/api-test/ must exist.

    2. Virtual-environment root directory: /home/user/api-test/env  
       Critical sub-items to be checked:  
       – /home/user/api-test/env/bin/activate (executable script)  
       – /home/user/api-test/env/bin/python (interpreter)

    3. /home/user/api-test/requirements.txt  
       Exact content (two lines, \n line endings):  
           httpx==0.25.1\n
           requests==2.31.0\n

    4. /home/user/api-test/install.log  
       Exact content (two lines, \n line endings):  
           httpx 0.25.1\n
           requests 2.31.0\n
